behaviors.description: Speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so summaries can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels". A lower Integration test with stubbed transcripts can pass even when the real STT diarization model collapses two speakers into one label, which breaks downstream attribution. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: this behavior depends on real model output content from STT, not just wrapper wiring. Rejected higher level (Workflow): the behavior text focuses on STT output preservation itself, not a required sequence of multiple model decisions for pass/fail.
behaviors.test_description: Run real diarized meeting audio through the real STT API and assert speaker-labeled utterances are preserved in the transcript artifact consumed by summarization (stable speaker IDs/names attached to lines). Use known multi-speaker fixtures where diarization mistakes are plausible.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: This test must include real STT model behavior; a fake transcript would hide the target failure.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote: "Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments... into the summary input structure." This is local parsing/structuring logic. Concrete miss at lower level: N/A (Unit is the floor). Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior text does not require cross-module handoff failure; it targets extraction logic itself.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function representative transcript lines with explicit assignments (e.g., "Alex will draft the Q3 plan") and assert exact structured output fields for assignee and task text, including edge punctuation and multiple assignments in one turn.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required to catch this parsing failure.

behaviors.description: Live summary contains at least one action item for every attendee assigned a task in the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task." A lower Integration test with mocked LLM responses can always satisfy coverage and miss real model omissions (e.g., model drops one assignee despite clear transcript evidence). LLM-DEP applies because correctness is about generation quality/content. Rejected higher level (Workflow): this behavior can be validated with one real summarization decision from prepared transcript input; it does not require multi-agentic pipeline sequencing in the test design.
behaviors.test_description: Use real meeting transcripts with multiple assignees, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output. Repeat across multiple runs/seeds to detect nondeterministic dropouts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The asserted property depends on real model output; deterministic stubs would mask omissions.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` schema section, not narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quote: "Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields." This is routing/mapping logic once decisions are already identified. Rejected higher level (Integration): no explicit module-boundary handoff failure is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke routing function with pre-identified decision objects plus narrative content and assert serialized summary places decisions only under `decisions` and not in narrative fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A single-function/object test is sufficient to catch misrouting logic defects.

behaviors.description: Key decisions in the transcript appear in live summary `decisions` section, not buried in narrative.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section." A lower Integration test with fixed mocked outputs misses real failures where the model narrates decisions but fails to place them in the structured section. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model extraction and placement behavior. Rejected higher level (Workflow): the behavior can be tested with one real summarization call over transcript text; no required multi-agentic sequence is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with explicit decision statements, call real summarization, and assert those decisions are represented in the `decisions` field (not only narrative text). Include paraphrased decision phrasing to test extraction robustness.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real model behavior is necessary; mock responses cannot reveal misplacement tendencies.

behaviors.description: For audio longer than 60 minutes, service segments audio, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary." Concrete miss at lower Agentic level: a single-call model test cannot catch cross-segment continuity failures (duplicate/conflicting action items, lost decisions at segment boundaries, broken attendee carryover) that appear only across sequential multi-step processing. MULTI-STEP applies explicitly due to chained segment processing plus merge.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through the production segmentation path, execute sequential STT/summarization per segment, then merge. Assert global coherence constraints: no segment-boundary loss of decisions/action items, consistent attendee attribution, and unified structured schema.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The target failure is in multi-step agentic handoff/merge across segments, which lower levels cannot faithfully expose.

behaviors.description: Full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes end-to-end without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording." Concrete miss at lower Agentic level: testing one model call cannot detect orchestration failures across the chained pipeline (upload-to-transcribe handoff, intermediate state persistence, retry/recovery between steps, final delivery contract). This is an explicit full multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting audio file through production-like interfaces: upload, real transcription, real summarization, and final structured output delivery. Assert completion without human intervention and validate final artifact schema/availability.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Because the behavior is explicitly pipeline-wide and multi-step with real model calls, Workflow is required.
